subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Why Rest Matters for Healing
description: A quick win: understand Why Rest Matters for Healing.

hook.text: Athletes who skip rest days after injury take twice as long to recover. Rest isn't laziness.

buildup.text: During rest, the body diverts energy from movement to tissue repair.

discovery.text: Growth hormone—essential for tissue repair—is released primarily during deep sleep.

twist.text: Stress hormones like cortisol actively slow wound healing, making anxiety a physical enemy.

climax.text: Studies show surgical patients who sleep well heal faster and need less pain medication.

punchline.text: Sleep isn't downtime. It's repair time.

quiz.question: When is growth hormone for tissue repair primarily released?
